raw_consumer_statement: On XXXX, I stayed at an XXXX  ( reservation code XXXX ). I was charged {$680.00} ( descriptor XXXX * XXXX ) via XXXX funded by my American Express card. The services were not as described and were unsafe : the AC did not work ( thermostat showed XXXX while set to XXXX ), the shower handle was missing, the bathroom/towels were dirty, several outlets emitted a burnt smell when used, and the TV did not work. Per a sign in the room instructing guests to contact the host or the front desk, I reported these issues in person multiple times ; I was told maintenance would come, but no one ever came and nothing was fixed. On XX/XX/year> I disputed the charge with American Express ( also provided the host listing showing working AC/TV and photos of the conditions ). Amex denied the dispute twice after the merchant submitted boilerplate about authorization, cancellation policy, and no in-app contact, without addressing my condition evidence or my in-person attempts to resolve as directed on site. I asked Amex to recode and re-review as services not as described/unsafe conditions, and to request maintenance/security logs, but the charge was rebilled. I am seeking CFPB assistance to have Amex re-review under the correct reason code and consider the photos/listing evidence demonstrating the unit was unsafe and not as advertised.
